title: pelargonium antidysentericum subsp. zonale
intro: Pelargonium antidysentericum subsp. zonale, also known as Zonal Geranium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Geraniaceae, native to South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and diarrhea, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Zonal Geranium include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from anxiety and stress. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, and is also used in aromatherapy due to its calming properties and distinct scent.
